description_long: This is a medium-sized wooden bucket with vertical wooden staves bound together by two dark metal hoops, one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood displays a natural grain pattern with some weathering and discoloration, suggesting outdoor use. The bucket features a sturdy metal bail handle attached to two wooden posts that extend above the rim, each with a metal eyelet for the handle to pivot. The interior appears hollow and unfinished, consistent with traditional water or utility buckets. The overall construction is robust, with visible metal fasteners reinforcing the structure. There are no visible labels, markings, or decorative elements, giving it a utilitarian appearance.
description: A weathered wooden bucket with metal hoops and a metal bail handle, showing natural wood grain and sturdy construction.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden staves, metal hoops, and one of the handle's attachment posts with a metal eyelet.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full arc of the metal bail handle and both wooden posts extending above the rim.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the opposite handle post and eyelet visible.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the curvature of the bucket and the metal handle spanning across the top.
